title: cephalanthera alpicola var. shizuoi
intro: Cephalanthera alpicola var. shizuoi, also known as Shizuoka alpine helleborine, is a rare and endangered species of orchid native to Japan, characterized by its delicate white flowers and slender green leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits associated with Cephalanthera alpicola var. shizuoi are said to include relief from fever, inflammation, and skin irritation, as well as its potential to boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions or decoctions, which can be consumed as teas or applied topically to affected areas.
